title: #eng-oncall: 8160000000
preview:
Maya Chen: hey team — unexpected flight change, stuck at SFO. my primary window ends in ~2.5h. can someone take primary for the last 2h? i can stay on shadow until wheels up if needed :airplane:
Ethan Park: @Maya Chen yep i can take the end of your shift. do you need me to run through any hot contexts?
Maya Chen: thanks so much. main things to watch: 1) embed pipeline errors (we saw a retry blip earlier), 2) latency spike on gen.prod.us-east-1 for large prompts. last incident had fallback to gn-medium which helped.
Maya Chen: runbook refs: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/prim-sec-handshake and the incident checklist: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/latency-spike-checklist
Aria (oncall-bot): Incident bot: new pager created — INC-9241 — gen.prod latency spike (p95 -> 1.9s). Pager pushed to primary Maya Chen, secondary Ethan Park. Pager link: https://pager.redwood/INC-9241
pagerbot: Pager: INC-9241 acknowledged by Maya Chen at 8160000060
Priya Singh: @Ethan Park if you're taking primary, quick note: autoscale policy for gen.prod has burst disabled in eu region; US has burst enabled. for this window, fallback policy is to route to gn-medium in us-east.
Ethan Park: got it. Maya, can you hand over current mitigation steps? any open canaries or ongoing rollouts?
Maya Chen: nothing rolling now. mitigation in place: prefix cache warm, 2 canaries of gn-large pinned to 1.4. we had a temporary KV cache miss—reconciled earlier but watch for a reappearance. i put a short snippet in the runbook:
```
# quick check list
curl -sS https://telemetry.redwood/api/latency?env=prod | jq .p95
redwoodctl infra scale --pool gen-prod --to 6
# rollback canary if p95 > 2.0 for >5m
```
Liam O'Connor: copying runbook step: if p95 > 2.2 and retries spike, openthrottle patch: `kubectl -n infra set env deploy/gen-proxy MAX_PENDING=200` and notify infra.
Ethan Park: i'll take primary at 8160000100. Maya, can you confirm you've handed over slack/channel privileges and updated oncall sheet?
Maya Chen: done — edited the sheet, added "Maya -> Ethan 2h handoff (flight)". also added short notes under the thread with the KV key prefixes to watch.
Ethan Park: ok taking primary. quick contact plan: if anything escalates I'll ping #eng-sre and @Priya Singh for autoscale help. Liam, can you be standby L2 for the next 45m?
Liam O'Connor: yep i can standby for 45m then i've got a meeting. i'll stay reachable for urgent pager escalation.
Priya Singh: i'll monitor autoscale and queue depths from my side. if we need to flip to gn-medium, i'll coordinate the rollout and update the console.
Aria (oncall-bot): Handoff recorded: Maya Chen -> Ethan Park; start 8160000100; expected end 81600008200. Note: please manually confirm when Maya disconnects.
Maya Chen: quick extra: customer impact — 2 customers (id: C-312, C-721) saw degraded perf earlier. i've emailed their CSMs and flagged for postmortem if we hit >5% error rate again.
Ethan Park: thanks — i'll add those customers to the post-incident note if we see regressions. taking pager into rotation now.
pagerbot: Pager: INC-9241 acknowledged by Ethan Park at 8160000110
Ethan Park: initial checks — p95 trending down to 1.5s after a short autoscale; watch for regressions. i'm going to keep the canary at gn-large for now for 15m.
Liam O'Connor: FYI ran `redwoodctl monitor --pool gen-prod --metric queue_depth` — no sustained growth.
Priya Singh: thumbs — i'll prepare the gn-medium fallback command in case. putting snippet here:
```
# fallback to gn-medium
redwoodctl model route set gen-prod --variant gn-medium --weight 100 --dry-run=false
```
Ethan Park: if p95 drops below 1.3 and stable for 10m i'll mark incident resolved and hand back to Maya after she checks in. Maya, safe travels. ping me if you want a final quick sync before gate. :thumbsup:
Maya Chen: cheers everyone — will message once wheels up. appreciate the quick pick up. :wave:

title: #eng-oncall: 7167800000
preview:
Marta (incident-bot): ⚠️ Incident summary for shift handoff — embeddings routing spikes seen in eu-west-1. Root page at 7167789000. Paging noisy but customer impact appears limited to ~4–6% of embedding requests. See brief below and actions taken.

Ava Chen: taking primary for the next 8h. I’m on call and will own escalations. Ravi — you ok to be secondary?

Ravi Patel: secondary, available. ping me if pages escalate. quick question: is this still only embeddings?

Diego Morales: yep, embeddings pipeline only. Latency p95 up from 120ms -> 520ms, and 429 rate increased in the egress router. CPU on the embedding workers trending high.

Jules - SRE: noticed autoscaler oscillating on embedding-deployment-3. Throttle / eviction logs show a tight loop:
```
2026-03-15T11:02:03Z embedding-worker[pid=1423]: eviction: cache_pressure=0.92
2026-03-15T11:02:03Z router: 429 respond - queue_len=410
```

Ava Chen: thanks. Current status: impact = partial degradation, affects embedding endpoints in eu-west-1 only, about 4–6% of requests either 429 or >500ms. No active customer escalations yet.

Ewa (oncall-helper): runbook refs: https://internal.redwood/runbooks/embeddings-autoscaler and https://internal.redwood/playbooks/egress-429. Starting checklist applied: scale up replica count, bump minReplicas=4 -> 8, pause autoscaler ramp-down.

Ravi Patel: applied minReplicas change via k8s patch. Command used:
```kubectl -n embeddings patch hpa embedding-deployment-3 -p '{"spec":{"minReplicas":8}}'```
responded OK.

Diego Morales: I put a temporary routing rule to divert low-priority embedding requests to model-emb-small (lower-cost, lower-latency) to reduce pressure. Rule live: route: embeddings.eu-west-1 -> prefer model-emb-large unless queue_len>300 then fallback to model-emb-small.

Ava Chen: good. Any signs of backend OOMs or node flaps?

Jules - SRE: no OOMs, but one node reported high swap usage. I cordoned node euw-gpu-12 to avoid further churn and drained pods to spread load.

Marta (incident-bot): Pager resolution timeline: page started 11:01 UTC; mitigations started 11:15 UTC; partial service relief 11:24 UTC.

Ewa (oncall-helper): I also enabled prefix KV cache warmup for the most common embedding keys to reduce recompute. This is a config toggle in feature-flags: embeddings.prefetch=true.

Ravi Patel: monitoring dashboards updated: embedding-p95 now 210ms, 429 rate down to 0.9% in eu-west-1. Still above baseline but improving.

Ava Chen: next steps for whoever picks this up later: 1) keep autoscaler minReplicas=8 for 2 hours, 2) watch for oscillation, 3) run heap/profile on embedding-worker-# if CPU stays high, 4) if 429s creep back above 2%, consider temporarily enabling global fallback to model-emb-small for all eu-west traffic.

Diego Morales: note for postmortem: autoscaler tuning keyset causes oscillation when cache-miss rate spikes. Dev-ml team to investigate quantization/prepare smaller model variant to reduce cold compute.

Jules - SRE: adding runbook pointer in the thread for bridge and follow-up: https://internal.redwood/runbooks/embeddings-postmortem-guidelines. Also leaving a TODO to improve alert thresholds to reduce noisy pages.

Ava Chen: handoff specifics: primary = Ava Chen, secondary = Ravi Patel. On-call bridge is open until 12:30 UTC for coverage if anyone needs synchronous sync. Current state = degraded -> recovering. Customer impact = limited, no major customers reported.

Ewa (oncall-helper): final checks I ran:
- Confirmed k8s patch applied and pods created (embedding-worker-8/8 up)
- Confirmed routing rule via feature-flags is active
- Verified no increase in errors in downstream fallback metrics

Ravi Patel: ack. I’ll stay on standby and can take over if Ava steps out. If you need an exec note prepared, I can draft a short update for support.

Ava Chen: thanks. If things look stable in 30m we’ll relax minReplicas to 6 and monitor for 1h. Leaving these action items in the thread:
- [Ava] monitor p95 and 429, adjust minReplicas down only if stable for 30m
- [Ravi] prepare customer comms draft in case escalations appear
- [Diego] follow up with ML infra on quantization/worker optimizations
- [Jules] schedule post-incident scrub for next oncall rotation

Marta (incident-bot): Incident closed to critical state at 11:40 UTC — still in monitoring. Page count: 7. Reopen if full regression. :white_check_mark:

Ava Chen: handing over context to next shift. If there’s regression, page primary/secondary and shout in #eng-oncall. handing over — Ava out. :wave:

Ravi Patel: taking notes, will pick up if needed. good handoff. :thumbsup:

Diego Morales: leaving logs and traces snippet here for later debug:
```
trace_id=abc123 span=encoder-latency  duration=480ms
sample_keys=[user:123, doc:kg-45, prompt:short]
```

Jules - SRE: added internal timeline and tag "embeddings-eu-west-03" to the incident tracker.

Ewa (oncall-helper): thanks all. Closing this handover thread.

Oncall Bot: Reminder — next on-call shift starts 12:00 UTC, please follow the pre-shift checklist: https://internal.redwood/checklists/oncall-preflight

title: #eng-oncall: 3587650000
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up — I'm putting together the on-call handoff plan for next weekend and coordinating shadowing for Priya (she starts shadowing this Sat). Need to confirm primary/secondary coverage and a short runthrough for the new shadow. Can folks call out availability?

Samir Patel: I can do primary Fri 18:00 -> Sat 06:00 if that helps.

Luca Rossi: I can take Sat 06:00 -> Sun 06:00 primary. Can be secondary Sun evening if needed.

Kim Nguyen: I'm out Sat afternoon, but can be secondary Fri night.

Marco Silva: I can be backup secondary Sun morning -> evening. Also happy to give Priya a 30m walkthrough Fri 16:00 (zoom).

Priya Nair: Thanks! I'm the new on-call shadow. I'd appreciate the walkthrough. Quick Q: what's expected of a shadow during a live page? Do I just observe or try to help?

Ava Chen: Priya — primarily observe and assist with documentation. Don't ack pages unless you and the primary agree. If you see anything obviously critical in logs, ping the primary thread + :phone: and we can escalate together. We'll go over the runbook items during the walkthrough.

Ava Chen: Handoff rules (short):
- Primary: receives pages, owns investigation, drives mitigation.
- Secondary: reachable within 10 minutes, can take over if primary needs a break or escalates.
- Shadow: observe, run sanity checks under guidance, take notes for post-incident review.

Ava Chen: Pager thresholds (copied from ops runbook):
- P0: Service-wide outage or data loss — page every 60s. Escalate immediately.
- P1: Significant degradation for core customers — page every 5m until ack.
- P2: Partial impact or non-critical degradation — page every 15m.

Ava Chen: Runbook & quick commands: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/oncall-handoff (bookmark this). Key snippets to know:
```bash
# quick check for GPU pool health
curl -sS https://infra-api.internal/metrics/gpu-health | jq .status
# fetch last 100 inference errors
redis-cli -h ops-redis LRANGE inference:errors -100
# restart api pod (only if instructed by SRE lead)
kubectl rollout restart deployment/api -n prod --request-timeout=30s
```

Ava Chen: Handoff checklist (to run during shift swap):
1) Confirm primary/secondary & contacts in the channel.
2) Share active incidents & status (runbook link + incident id).
3) Check recent deploys (DeployBot message).
4) Validate paging rules & alert suppression windows.
5) Confirm who is shadowing and pairing time.

DeployBot: deploy finished: service/api v2.14.1 -> prod by @ci — 2024-10-11T14:12Z :white_check_mark:

Samir Patel: Noted. I saw the deploy earlier, will verify health checks at shift start.

Luca Rossi: @Priya I'll add you to my zoom for 10:00 Sat so you can watch a mock page drill and try the checklist.

Priya Nair: Thanks @Luca — appreciate it. Also wondering about escalation path for customer pages — do we notify support/AE?

Ava Chen: Good Q. Escalation path:
- Primary owns technical mitigation.
- If customer-visible incident (P0/P1), primary or secondary posts to #incidents with RCA-owner and triage summary, and NOTIFY support + AE on-call via the incident template. Use the incident template in the runbook.

Ava Chen: I'll assign formal shifts here:
- Fri 18:00 -> Sat 06:00: Primary = Samir Patel, Secondary = Kim Nguyen, Shadow = none
- Sat 06:00 -> Sun 06:00: Primary = Luca Rossi, Secondary = Marco Silva, Shadow = Priya Nair (shadow window 10:00-14:00)
- Sun 06:00 -> Mon 06:00: Primary = TBD (volunteers?), Secondary = Marco Silva

Kim Nguyen: I can cover Sun 06:00 -> Sun 18:00 primary if no one else steps up.

Marco Silva: I can switch to secondary Sun night if Kim takes primary.

Ava Chen: Great — updated schedule above. Priya, we'll do the walkthrough Fri 16:00 (15m) then Sat 10:00 (mock drill). Please review the runbook before Fri: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/oncall-handoff

Priya Nair: Will do. One last ask — any tips for dealing with noisy alerts during onboarding?

Samir Patel: Mute non-actionable alert channels for your watch. Use the alerts dashboard filter to only show P0/P1. If you see a noisy alert that isn't actionable, create a ticket to tune it.

Ava Chen: Also: if a noisy alert floods during shadowing, primary can pause the shadow and step through the incident with them after containment. We don't want to overwhelm new folks.

IncidentBot: [TEST PAGE] Manual page test by @Ava Chen — P1 — check-in from 3587650055 :bell:

Ava Chen: That test page was intentional to make sure Priya can see how pages arrive. Priya — don't ack test pages.

Priya Nair: Saw it — thanks. I saw the alert payload and logs, looks straightforward.

Samir Patel: Ok all set from my side. If anything pops up before Fri handoff I'll post updates here.

Ava Chen: Thanks everyone. I'll post the canonical schedule in the on-call calendar and pin the runbook link here. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-oncall: 3071237890
preview:
Maya Patel: flagging a recurring problem: postmortem items are landing in backlog and not getting verified. Proposing a compact remediation schema + automated reminders + a stricter escalation ladder. Short discussion?

Diego Ruiz: yes please. the fewer free-text fields the better. also we should require a 'regression-check' that is automated (smoke test) so we don't rely on manual signoff.

Lena Ho: agree on automation. also we keep seeing the same failure modes (db-connection, cache-evict, auth-timeouts). can we tag those on the PM and feed a weekly digest to eng leads?

Maya Patel: sketch of a tiny YAML we can paste into a Jira comment — parseable and enforces minimal fields:

```
remediation: "short sentence"
owner: "Name (team)"
priority: "P0|P1|P2"
due: "YYYY-MM-DD"
failure_mode: [db-connection, cache-evict, infra-deg]
prevented_by: "what guardrail prevents recurrence"
regression_check: "smoke-suite-id or url"
checkpoints: "36h:status, 3d:status, 21d:status"
pm_link: "postmortem url"
notes: "optional"
```

Ben Carter: Nice. I like the 36h checkpoint (faster than 72h). Ops-bot can ping the owner and the channel if a checkpoint value is unchanged.

Diego Ruiz: escalation idea: if 36h missed -> notify owner + team channel; if 3d missed -> escalate to tech lead and auto-create follow-up card in remediation board; if 21d no regression_check -> open a bug and tag for backlog review.

Lena Ho: For IC rotation: propose 1-week IC shifts with a daytime shadow for the last day that does handoff + a one-pager checklist. IC should pick comms cadence on declare: minimal template: initial, 30m, major-update, final. Keep updates under 3 lines and link to timeline.

Maya Patel: Also: require a recurrence assessment field: "short-term fix? yes/no" and "long-term action? yes/no" so we can prioritize systemic work. If long-term=yes, assign a follow-up owner and schedule a triage with platform in 7d window.

Ben Carter: operational notes: ops-bot can support reminders, tag extracts, and weekly heatmap. I can wire a small lambda to scan Jira comments for the YAML and emit metrics.

ops-bot: Reminder created: checkpoint 36h for REM-2024-455 (owner: Maya Patel) will post in #eng-oncall at 3071239290.

Diego Ruiz: governance q: who enforces SLA? suggestion: team lead owns enforcement; IC owns the immediate remediation pacing during incident -> to avoid split responsibilities.

Lena Ho: also add a tiny field: "verification: automated/manual" so dashboard can surface what still needs human follow-up.

- Ben: prototype YAML extractor + weekly failure-mode digest (owner: Ben, due: 10 days)
- Lena: write the IC 1-week shift handoff + one-pager checklist (owner: Lena, due: 4 days)
- Diego: draft escalation ladder and a short runbook paragraph for SLA enforcement (owner: Diego, due: 3 days)
- Maya: update REM-2024-455 with the YAML and trigger the 36h checkpoint (owner: Maya, due: today)

title: #eng-oncall: 2890012345
preview:
incident-bot: PAGER: High error rate detected for Dedicated pool dp-123 in us-west-2. 5xx spike + queue length > 120s. Threshold breached at 2026-03-15T21:07:25Z. Paging on-call.
Ava Chen: ack I'm on it, looking at metrics now.
Ravi Patel: I just woke up, taking primary. Can someone paste the top offenders?
Elena Gomez: throwing up quick dashboard links: https://monitoring.redwood.io/dp-123/overview — latency and inflight look scary. Most requests are from api keys for ACME and BetaCorp.
Kai Matsumoto: logs show repeated KV cache misses + long tail (>20s) on tokenization step for many short requests.
Samir Khan: hypothesis: autoscaler didn't spawn burst workers due to a config mismatch on dp-123 (min_replicas=2, burst_enabled=false).
Ava Chen: if burst_enabled is false we should enable burst capacity now. Ravi do you want me to toggle?
Ravi Patel: yes please. Also start temporary rate limit on heavy customers while we flip burst.
Ava Chen: running command to enable burst capacity (will expire in 30m unless extended):
```bash
# enable burst for Dedicated pool dp-123
redwoodctl dedicated pools update --pool-id dp-123 --set burst_enabled=true --set burst_duration=30m
```
ops-bot: ACTION: update request received for pool dp-123 by Ava Chen. Job queued (job_id: jb-7723).
Ava Chen: job completed. burst_enabled=true, burst_nodes=8 added. Monitoring now.
Kai Matsumoto: seeing CPU nodes come up and KV hit rate improving. Latency dropping from 1.6s p95 -> 600ms p95. Nice.
Ravi Patel: starting targeted rate-limits for ACME and BetaCorp for next 15m:
```http
PATCH /admin/tenants/ACME/limits
Content-Type: application/json
{ "rate_limit": { "requests_per_minute": 300 } }
```
Elena Gomez: sent to prod-configs api, response 200.
Customer Success (Maya): Maya here — I messaged ACME account team: we told them we're doing a temporary throttling to stabilize infra, expect <10% degradation, will notify when lifted.
Samir Khan: also toggled streaming to best-effort for all Dedicated pools to reduce per-connection overhead. Command: `redwoodctl dedicated config set --pool dp-123 --streaming mode=best-effort`
Ava Chen: checking error rates now. 5xx counts down 82% in last 5m. Queue time <30s. Good.
Ravi Patel: marking mitigation successful for now. We still need root cause — why burst_enabled=false?
Nina Park: copying config history: dp-123 was created with template stable-v2 on 2025-11-02; a recent infra migration on 2026-02-28 accidentally set burst_enabled=false on pools using stable-v2. Patch in infra repo open: gh/infra#842.
Ava Chen: ok note that in incident thread. Also need to confirm we didn't exceed dedicated commit limits while adding burst nodes. Finance alert?
Samir Khan: burst was within allowance for emergency bursts per contract; ops-bot confirmed no billing alert triggered.
Ravi Patel: prepare for handoff — I'm going to bed in 20m. Ava can you own next 2-hour watch and run a short postmortem draft?
Ava Chen: yes. I'll keep monitoring for 30m, then hand off to Elena at 00:00 UTC.
Elena Gomez: acknowledged. I'll compile the timeline and add ACME impact estimate.
Ava Chen: customer impact: ACME saw errors for ~12 minutes, approx 1.8k failed requests total. BetaCorp minor (<200 fails); other customers unaffected.
Customer Success (Maya): I've queued a proactive message to both customers offering status and ETA.
Ravi Patel: adding follow-ups: 1) infra migration rollback rule to preserve burst flag 2) add alert to detect pool with high queue + burst_disabled 3) run chaos test to ensure autoscaler spawns nodes under spike.
Nina Park: created follow-up tickets: INFRA-1123, ALERTS-778, CHAOS-55.
Ava Chen: handing off to Elena in 10m. Leaving note: burst was a valid emergency knob, but we should avoid manual forever toggles.
Elena Gomez: taking over. Will escalate to Platform PM for config template change.
Ravi Patel: final status before sleep: mitigated via burst enable + targeted rate-limits + streaming downgrade. Recommend 72hr RCA with logs and exact timing. Good job team. :thumbsup:
incident-bot: INCIDENT: dp-123 high-error resolved. Duration 00:28:55. Severity: Sev2. Postmortem link draft: https://docs.redwood.internal/incidents/dp-123-burst-mitigation-2890012345 (draft)
Ava Chen: will fill postmortem and include exact CLI commands & metrics graphs.
Elena Gomez: will own customer communications follow-through.
Nina Park: logging handoff in oncall rotation: next oncall - Kai.
Kai Matsumoto: acknowledged, will keep an eye overnight.
Ava Chen: thanks all.
Ravi Patel: good night.

title: #eng-oncall: 2370012345
preview:
Priya Raman: Hey folks — need a short-notice night shift swap for tomorrow night (00:00-08:00 PST). Family emergency, sorry for the late ask. I can hand off everything but can't stay on call. I will prewarm and leave notes. TL;DR: prewarm g4dn-pool, avoid deploys to model-service, watch the small-q queue (queue-small) for growth.

Sam Ortiz: I can take the first half (00:00-04:00), but need someone to cover 04:00-08:00.

Ava Chen: I can pick up 04:00-08:00. Thanks for the heads up — hope everything's okay. :hugging_face:

Diego Morales: Quick q — any planned batch jobs around 01:00 that will spike traffic? I can pause them if needed.

Priya Raman: Thanks Sam/Ava — much appreciated. Diego: yes, there's a nightly embedding-batch at 01:10 but it's low priority; please postpone if the queue starts building. I will disable auto-retry for that job before I leave.

Priya Raman: Handoff notes (short):
- Prewarm: run on infra host ->
```
# prewarm command
redis-cli -n 2 LPUSH prewarm:models "gpt-medium-v1" "gpt-medium-v1-qa"
infractl prewarm --pool g4dn --models gpt-medium-v1 --timeout 600
```
- Critical services: model-service, routing-proxy, kv-cache.
- Avoid any rolling deploys to model-service between 23:45 and 08:30 PST.
- Expected load: steady, ~120 req/min; cache hit ~60%. Watch `queue-small` > 300 -> escalate.

Sam Ortiz: Please post the short escalation path and PD contacts. Also, any specific log snippets to look for?

Priya Raman: PagerDuty: @sre-oncall and @infra-lead. Escalation steps: 1) check `routing-proxy` metrics (errors > 0.5% for 5m) 2) check `model-service` OOMs -> restart pod with 120s backoff 3) if persistent, escalate to infra-lead.
Log grep to check failures: `kubectl logs -l app=model-service -n prod | grep "OOM|error" -A 5`

oncall-bot: Calendar updated: Priya Raman -> Sam Ortiz (00:00-04:00), Ava Chen (04:00-08:00).

Ava Chen: Got it. One more: token-limit threshold for the hosted API? I remember you had a per-tenant cap.

Priya Raman: per-tenant token cap is 10M/day; threshold alert at 9M. For this night, no tenant is near that but if traffic surges check `tenant-stats` dashboard and mute background retrains.

Diego Morales: I'll also set a temporary alert on `queue-small` > 200 to give us earlier notice.

Sam Ortiz: Thanks, Priya. I'll take first watch and post a quick handoff message 30m before 00:00 with live state.

Priya Raman: Thanks all. Posting the last thing before I sign off: runbook link -> https://confluence.internal/runbooks/oncall/night-handoff and the mini checklist: "prewarm done, disable embed-job auto-retry, post to this thread when you take over, escalate to @sre-oncall if queue-small > 300". Appreciate you both.

Ava Chen: Noted. See you on the thread, will ping if I see anything odd.

Sam Ortiz: Will confirm in-thread when I take over. Good luck, Priya.

Priya Raman: Thank you — transferring handoff in 30m. :pray:

title: #eng-oncall: 2240012345
preview:
Incident Bot: Paging: elevated token TPS and error 429s observed for model family 'nebula-13b' in us-west-2. Spike started at ~22:10 UTC, sustained. Alerts: per-model TPS > 4x expected, queue depth increasing :rotating_light:
Alex Chen: ack. I'm oncall. digging in.
Mira Patel: i'm loading recent metric dashboards — looks like a small subset of customers are hitting long-chat workloads, generating very large prefixes. TPM and RPM both high for nebula-13b.
Alex Chen: confirm 429s are coming from rate limits or from serving backpressure?
Mira Patel: quick check: origin is quota guard hitting per-model RPM caps and then triggering 429. Upstream serving latency also climbing.
Alex Chen: ok. We need a live mitigation plan: reduce per-model RPM for nebula-13b, enable fallback routing to nebula-8b, and temporarily disable streaming to reduce KV memory pressure. Thoughts?
SRE Lead: agree with that order. Start with RPM/TMP change (least customer-visible), then streaming toggle. We should document customer impact before changes.
Alex Chen: copying mitigation commands — using rdwctl (internal ops).
Alex Chen: ```bash
# emergency throttle to reduce per-model RPM/TPM for nebula-13b
rdwctl quotas set --model nebula-13b --tpm 120 --rpm 600 --note "emergency-surge-2240012345"
```
Ops Bot: ran: rdwctl quotas set returned job_id=job-99321 queued. Expected propagation ~15s.
Mira Patel: running quick canary: simulate a large prefix using loadtool against nebula-13b in staging. Waiting for quota to apply.
Alex Chen: also preparing fallback route: route policy to prefer nebula-8b for non-critical tiers.```yaml
# route update snippet
- match: model.family == "nebula-13b" and customer.tier != "enterprise"
  action: route-to: nebula-8b
  rollback: true
```
Jordan Blake: are we notifying any customers? Which ones are impacted?
Mira Patel: I pulled logs, top 5 customer IDs by token usage in last 10m: cust-402, cust-117, cust-909, cust-221, cust-008. Mix of dev/prod. cust-909 is enterprise but with high long-chat usage.
Alex Chen: draft notice: "We're temporarily reducing per-model throughput for nebula-13b to mitigate capacity degradation. Some requests may see increased latency or be routed to compatible models. We expect this to last < 2 hours while we scale pools." Approve? :thumbsup: / :thumbsdown:
SRE Lead: +1. Send via status page + targeted DM to cust-909 and cust-221.
Incident Bot: quota change propagated at 22:12:08 UTC (prop_id=job-99321).
Mira Patel: initial telemetry: per-model RPM dropped 65%, 429s from quota guard falling. Serving latency still slightly elevated at 95th percentile. Memory pressure on kv-cache improving.
Alex Chen: next step: disable streaming for nebula-13b to reduce KV churning. Command:```bash
rdwctl feature toggle --model nebula-13b --feature streaming --state off --reason "reduce-kv-pressure-emergency"
```
Ops Bot: feature toggle queued job_id=feat-44211; propagation ~10s.
Jordan Blake: what customer visible change will disabling streaming cause?
Mira Patel: responses will be returned as single payloads rather than chunked. Some SDKs may show delay until full completion, but overall fewer partial failures. Need to call out in notice.
Alex Chen: executing streaming toggle now.
Incident Bot: feature toggle applied at 22:13:02 UTC (feat-44211).
Mira Patel: monitoring: latency 95p down 20% in last 5m, kv-cache pressure normalized. 429s near zero.
Alex Chen: pulling conf audit to capture exact changes for handoff. Patch: per-model tpm/rpm change and streaming off.
Alex Chen: ```text
Mitigation summary (2240012345):
- Reduced nebula-13b TPM to 120 and RPM to 600 at 22:12:08 UTC (job-99321).
- Disabled streaming for nebula-13b at 22:13:02 UTC (feat-44211).
- Route policy updated to prefer nebula-8b for non-enterprise customers at 22:14:10 UTC (policy-apply-778).
- Immediate effect: 429s due to quota guards dropped to baseline, kv-cache pressure reduced, 95p latency improved ~20%.
- Impacted customers: cust-402 (dev), cust-117 (prod), cust-909 (enterprise) — targeted DM sent to cust-909 and cust-221.
- Estimated user-impact window: 30m - 2h depending on capacity scale.
```
SRE Lead: file a post-incident ticket with timeline and follow-up: scale nebula-13b serving pool + consider autoscale policy tweak for long-chat prefixes.
Jordan Blake: i'm taking handoff for next 4h. My notes: watch for error resurfacing after quota relax, rollback plan: rdwctl quotas set --model nebula-13b --tpm 600 --rpm 3000 --note "rollback-if-stable-2240012345" (requires coordinated rollout). Also re-enable streaming only after kv-cache and kernel memory are stable.
Alex Chen: handoff ack. Jordan, I'll DM you credentials for the private escalation channel.
Mira Patel: schedule: we'll run controlled ramp of capacity in 30m, if QPS stays under thresholds, begin gradual RPM increase (30% steps every 10m).
Ops Bot: DM notification sent to cust-909 and cust-221 at 22:15:05 UTC.
Jordan Blake: noted. I'll keep the channel open and will escalate to infra if latency doesn't normalize in 45m.
Alex Chen: final quick checklist before leaving: confirm status page updated, incident ticket created, targeted customer DMs sent, handoff done.
SRE Lead: confirm all. Good job team. We'll keep this thread pinned for the postmortem. :white_check_mark:
Jordan Blake: taking over. leave any logs/queries you want me to run in the thread and i'll pick up.
Mira Patel: attaching two quick queries for handoff (copy/paste):```sql
-- top token usage by customer last 15m
select customer_id, sum(tokens) as tokens from request_logs where model_family='nebula-13b' and ts > now() - interval '15 minutes' group by customer_id order by tokens desc limit 10;
-- 95p latency by model shard
select shard, percentile_cont(0.95) within group (order by latency_ms) as p95 from serving_metrics where model_family='nebula-13b' and ts > now() - interval '15 minutes' group by shard;
```
Jordan Blake: got it. thanks. I'll update thread in 20m with status.
Alex Chen: leaving channel. thanks team. :wave:
Incident Bot: thread marked for follow-up (incident-2240012345).

Alex Chen: confirm 429s are coming from rate limits or from serving backpressure?
Mira Patel: quick check: origin is quota guard hitting per-model RPM caps and then triggering 429. Upstream serving latency also climbing.
Alex Chen: ok. We need a live mitigation plan: reduce per-model RPM for nebula-13b, enable fallback routing to nebula-8b, and temporarily disable streaming to reduce KV memory pressure. Thoughts?
SRE Lead: agree with that order. Start with RPM/TMP change (least customer-visible), then streaming toggle. We should document customer impact before changes.
Alex Chen: copying mitigation commands — using rdwctl (internal ops).
Alex Chen: ```bash
# emergency throttle to reduce per-model RPM/TPM for nebula-13b
rdwctl quotas set --model nebula-13b --tpm 120 --rpm 600 --note "emergency-surge-2240012345"
```
Ops Bot: ran: rdwctl quotas set returned job_id=job-99321 queued. Expected propagation ~15s.
Mira Patel: running quick canary: simulate a large prefix using loadtool against nebula-13b in staging. Waiting for quota to apply.
Alex Chen: also preparing fallback route: route policy to prefer nebula-8b for non-critical tiers.```yaml
# route update snippet
- match: model.family == "nebula-13b" and customer.tier != "enterprise"
  action: route-to: nebula-8b
  rollback: true
```
Jordan Blake: are we notifying any customers? Which ones are impacted?
Mira Patel: I pulled logs, top 5 customer IDs by token usage in last 10m: cust-402, cust-117, cust-909, cust-221, cust-008. Mix of dev/prod. cust-909 is enterprise but with high long-chat usage.
Alex Chen: draft notice: "We're temporarily reducing per-model throughput for nebula-13b to mitigate capacity degradation. Some requests may see increased latency or be routed to compatible models. We expect this to last < 2 hours while we scale pools." Approve? :thumbsup: / :thumbsdown:
SRE Lead: +1. Send via status page + targeted DM to cust-909 and cust-221.
Incident Bot: quota change propagated at 22:12:08 UTC (prop_id=job-99321).
Mira Patel: initial telemetry: per-model RPM dropped 65%, 429s from quota guard falling. Serving latency still slightly elevated at 95th percentile. Memory pressure on kv-cache improving.
Alex Chen: next step: disable streaming for nebula-13b to reduce KV churning. Command:```bash
rdwctl feature toggle --model nebula-13b --feature streaming --state off --reason "reduce-kv-pressure-emergency"
```
Ops Bot: feature toggle queued job_id=feat-44211; propagation ~10s.
Jordan Blake: what customer visible change will disabling streaming cause?
Mira Patel: responses will be returned as single payloads rather than chunked. Some SDKs may show delay until full completion, but overall fewer partial failures. Need to call out in notice.
Alex Chen: executing streaming toggle now.
Incident Bot: feature toggle applied at 22:13:02 UTC (feat-44211).
Mira Patel: monitoring: latency 95p down 20% in last 5m, kv-cache pressure normalized. 429s near zero.
Alex Chen: pulling conf audit to capture exact changes for handoff. Patch: per-model tpm/rpm change and streaming off.
Alex Chen: ```text
Mitigation summary (2240012345):
- Reduced nebula-13b TPM to 120 and RPM to 600 at 22:12:08 UTC (job-99321).
- Disabled streaming for nebula-13b at 22:13:02 UTC (feat-44211).
- Route policy updated to prefer nebula-8b for non-enterprise customers at 22:14:10 UTC (policy-apply-778).
- Immediate effect: 429s due to quota guards dropped to baseline, kv-cache pressure reduced, 95p latency improved ~20%.
- Impacted customers: cust-402 (dev), cust-117 (prod), cust-909 (enterprise) — targeted DM sent to cust-909 and cust-221.
- Estimated user-impact window: 30m - 2h depending on capacity scale.
```
SRE Lead: file a post-incident ticket with timeline and follow-up: scale nebula-13b serving pool + consider autoscale policy tweak for long-chat prefixes.
Jordan Blake: i'm taking handoff for next 4h. My notes: watch for error resurfacing after quota relax, rollback plan: rdwctl quotas set --model nebula-13b --tpm 600 --rpm 3000 --note "rollback-if-stable-2240012345" (requires coordinated rollout). Also re-enable streaming only after kv-cache and kernel memory are stable.
Alex Chen: handoff ack. Jordan, I'll DM you credentials for the private escalation channel.
Mira Patel: schedule: we'll run controlled ramp of capacity in 30m, if QPS stays under thresholds, begin gradual RPM increase (30% steps every 10m).
Ops Bot: DM notification sent to cust-909 and cust-221 at 22:15:05 UTC.
Jordan Blake: noted. I'll keep the channel open and will escalate to infra if latency doesn't normalize in 45m.
Alex Chen: final quick checklist before leaving: confirm status page updated, incident ticket created, targeted customer DMs sent, handoff done.
SRE Lead: confirm all. Good job team. We'll keep this thread pinned for the postmortem. :white_check_mark:
Jordan Blake: taking over. leave any logs/queries you want me to run in the thread and i'll pick up.
Mira Patel: attaching two quick queries for handoff (copy/paste):```sql
-- top token usage by customer last 15m
select customer_id, sum(tokens) as tokens from request_logs where model_family='nebula-13b' and ts > now() - interval '15 minutes' group by customer_id order by tokens desc limit 10;
-- 95p latency by model shard
select shard, percentile_cont(0.95) within group (order by latency_ms) as p95 from serving_metrics where model_family='nebula-13b' and ts > now() - interval '15 minutes' group by shard;
```
Jordan Blake: got it. thanks. I'll update thread in 20m with status.
Alex Chen: leaving channel. thanks team. :wave:
Incident Bot: thread marked for follow-up (incident-2240012345).

title: #eng-oncall: 2151234567
preview:
Ava Chen: I need to swap primary tonight 21:00->07:00 PT. I'm on a delayed flight. Can someone confirm they can take primary for that window?
Samir Patel: I can take primary 21:00-03:00 PT, but I have a meeting at 01:00 PT. Happy to be primary until 03:00 and then hand to Diego if he's able.
Diego Martinez: I can pick up 03:00-07:00 PT. Will be available to respond to pages.
Ava Chen: Thanks both. Confirming plan: Samir primary 21:00-03:00, Diego primary 03:00-07:00. Maya you're secondary for whole window?
Maya Li: Yes, I'll be secondary 21:00-07:00 PT. I'll keep the incident dashboard open and monitor error budget.
Ava Chen: Quick reminders for handover: 1) Check incident-bot for active pages. 2) Run this quick smoke check against the production inference gateway: ```curl -s -X POST https://api.redwood-inference.com/v1/healthz -H 'Authorization: Bearer $ONCALL_TOKEN' | jq .``` 3) If you see latency > 400ms p50, follow the 'latency-spike' runbook (links below).
Samir Patel: Copy. Where is the runbook?
Ava Chen: Runbook link: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/latency-spike and the paging policy: https://redwood.internal/oncall/paging-policy. Also note: embeddings shard 3 sometimes flakes under burst. If you get continuous 5xx from /v1/embeddings that's likely shard-3.
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Pager triggered: P1 - prod-embeddings QPS surge (pagerduty #INC-8243). Affected customers: yes (upsell tier). Triggered at 20:42 PT. Current status: investigating.
Samir Patel: Seen the incident-bot alert. I'm already on the call with infra. ETA to mitigation ~12 mins. I'll assume primary early and keep everyone posted.
Diego Martinez: I'll be on standby and join the call if escalation continues past 21:30 PT.
Maya Li: If Samir needs relief mid-incident, Diego can claim primary by typing `/oncall takeover 2151234567` (devex helper). Also make sure to post a short public note in #incidents when switching.
Samir Patel: On the call: root cause looks like a mis-routed KV cache invalidation that caused hot recompile on one of the embedding workers. We're throttling new requests to that shard and routing 20% to fallback quantized model.
Ava Chen: Good mitigation plan. Samir, when you hand off at 03:00 PT: 1) post in this thread that you're handing off, 2) confirm no active P1s, 3) run `kubectl get pods -n inference -l shard=3` and paste pod statuses.
Samir Patel: Will do. If the P1 is still active at 03:00 I'll extend to 04:00 and ping Diego.
Diego Martinez: Sounds good. One ask for handover: Samir, please dump current top traces to S3 with the command in the runbook so I can pick up right away.
Samir Patel: Yep running the trace dump now: ```aws s3 cp /tmp/trace-2151234567.json s3://redwood-oncall-dumps/trace-2151234567.json``` 
Maya Li: Logging the coverage: primary rota updated in PagerDuty notes and calendar invite updated. I'll also add a quick checklist in the handoff doc: https://redwood.internal/oncall/handoffs/night-standby
Ava Chen: Thanks team. Final check: anyone unable to respond to a page during their window? If not, I'll mark the swap in the schedule. :thumbsup:
Diego Martinez: all good from me.
Samir Patel: good to go.
Ava Chen: Swap confirmed. I'll update the schedule and set an auto-ack reminder for 20:55 PT before you go primary, Samir. Good luck and ping if anything changes. :raised_hands:

title: #eng-oncall: 2141594321
preview:
OncallBot: :rotating_light: INCIDENT INC-3821 (P1) — spike in /v1/generate 5xxs detected. Pager triggered to @eng-oncall. Link: https://go.redwood/incidents/INC-3821

Ava Chen: taking the 09:00-17:00 shift now. I see INC-3821 is active — Priya, are you still driving this?

Priya Nair: yes I'm on it, been triaging for ~18m. Root cause looks like OOM on one of the dedicated pools during a burst of quantized llama requests. Working hypothesis: an autoscaler race + a model variant with higher memory.

Ava Chen: ok — I'm taking comms if that's ok. Priya, can you keep driving mitigation and call out if you need a hand? I'm pulling the runbook and will post links. :wave:

Priya Nair: fine with that. quick notes: cluster rtx-prod-us-west-2 node group gpud-2 has two pods in CrashLoopBackOff. Logs show KV cache allocation failing.

Diego Rivera: heads up, I'm on the road but reachable by phone. I can be secondary for driver if needed. Not on VPN right now tho. :phone:

Ava Chen: noted — so ownership: Priya driving, Diego secondary standby, Ava comms. If Priya needs to escalate to Dedicated team we loop in @eng-runtime.

Sam Ortiz: I'm available to draft the incident summary for customers and PS if we confirm customer-facing impact. What is current customer impact?

Priya Nair: impact: ~12% of requests to /v1/generate returned 5xx in last 12m; error rate concentrated on requests hitting model redwood/llama-quant-8bit@v2 with sequence length > 1024. No broad auth failures.

Ava Chen: thanks. probable customer impact: degraded generation for some large-seq workloads, potential latency spikes. Sam, please prepare a short note — "We’re investigating elevated 5xx errors for large-generation requests; mitigation underway". I'll post to #customer-status once we have mitigation steps.

Priya Nair: mitigation steps I'm running now (from runbook https://go.redwood/runbooks/p1-api-errors):
```
# temporarily pin traffic off the problematic model variant
redwood route update /v1/generate --model redwood/llama-quant-8bit@v2 --weight 0.0
# scale up autoscaler min for gpud-2
kubectl -n inference scale deployment/gpud-2-autoscaler --replicas=4
# apply memory guard on model container
kubectl -n inference set env deploy/llama-quant --env="MODEL_MEM_GUARD=1"
```
I'll run the route update first and watch error rate.

Diego Rivera: if route update drops success rate elsewhere, be ready to rollback. Also check cache eviction metrics — prefix caching may be causing memory pressure.

Ava Chen: Priya, when you execute the route update, post the command output and short status summary here. Sam, draft posted customer note and I'll sign off. Diego, stand by for escalation.

Priya Nair: ran route update — returned 200, traffic rebalanced. Monitoring: error rate down to 1.9% (from 12%). Logs show fewer OOM traces. Still seeing sample pod crashes but not increasing.

Sam Ortiz: draft for #customer-status: "We are investigating elevated 5xx errors affecting a subset of large-generation requests. Mitigation in progress; we have temporarily routed traffic away from a model variant and are monitoring. We will provide an update in 30 minutes or sooner." Want me to post? :eyes:

Ava Chen: post that, please. Add link to incident page. :thumbsup:

OncallBot: Incident INC-3821 status updated to mitigation in progress by Priya Nair. Assigned roles: driver=Priya Nair, comms=Ava Chen, secondary=Diego Rivera.

Priya Nair: continuing to watch. next steps: let autoscaler settle for 5m, check pod restart frequency, if crashes continue I'll open a debug shell on the failing node and capture core dump.

Ava Chen: ok team — I'll take notes for the handoff at 13:00 if this is still open. If everything stabilizes, we'll close the incident and sync a postmortem owner. Thanks everyone. :heart:

title: #eng-oncall: 2059876543
preview:
oncall-bot: [ALERT] P1 - spike in 5xx rate on /v1/generate (eu-west-1). Paging #eng-oncall. Brief: error-rate ~12% over last 3m, latency stable.
Ava Chen: I’m taking initial driver. I’ll start by checking the service pods and recent deploys.
Samir Patel: I’m secondary. Can take comms if you want. Priya is out today so we have a small coverage gap for observability deep-dive.
Jordan Lee: I’m mid-release but can join in 10–15m if needed. If it’s traffic-related we should check the routing policy and any recent kernel changes.
Ava Chen: quick checks: kubectl get pods -n inference | grep model-server -> nothing OOMing. No recent deploys in the last 30m per deploy-bot.
Ava Chen: Running tail on logs and a quick request repro:
```
curl -s -X POST https://eu-west1-redwood/api/v1/generate -d '{"prompt":"hello"}' -H 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' | jq .
```
Shows intermittent 502 with backend error "kv cache mismatch" on a subset of instances.
Samir Patel: Putting a brief status for customers: "We’re investigating intermittent errors on generation endpoints in eu-west-1. Engineers are actively triaging; no data loss expected. Updates every 15m or on significant change." Posting now to status.redwood.
Ava Chen: Proposed roles: Driver = Ava Chen (triage + mitigation), Comms = Samir Patel (status updates + customer comms), Backup = Jordan Lee (escalation + infra). Anyone object?
Jordan Lee: ok by me. If you need me to ssh into the node I can in ~12m.
Priya Das: (offline, but leaving a note) If kv-cache mismatch persists, try clearing prefix cache on the affected pool and revert to warm cache mode per runbook.
Ava Chen: Good call. Runbook pointer: https://internal.redwood/runbooks/generation-5xx-triage. Key steps I’ll follow:
1) Identify affected instance IDs (logs show inst-53x, inst-598)
2) Isolate traffic to healthy pool via feature toggle or routing policy
3) Clear prefix/KV cache on affected instances and restart model server
4) Monitor error rate and rollback quantization change if correlated

Snippet from runbook I may use:
```
# Clear KV cache
ssh node-X
sudo systemctl stop model-server
rm -rf /var/lib/kv_cache/prefix_*
sudo systemctl start model-server
```
Samir Patel: I’ve posted the initial note on status.redwood and added the slack banner pinned in #customers-updates. Will send a short thread to the customer-facing channel with ETA ~15m.
Ava Chen: I’m going to redirect traffic off inst-53x now and clear cache on inst-598. Will update after each action.
Ava Chen: (update) traffic drain initiated for inst-53x via routing toggle -> traffic shows 0 req/s on that instance.
Ava Chen: cleared cache and restarted model-server on inst-598. Watching metrics.
Samir Patel: Current status for customers: "We’ve identified a subset of instances showing backend errors and have isolated them. Error rate declining for most customers; investigating root cause. Next update in 10m."
Jordan Lee: Error rate looks halved on the dashboard. If it holds, we can keep driver on monitoring and prepare a formal post-incident note.
Ava Chen: Error rate down to ~1.2% from 12% — looks stable for now. I’ll continue monitoring for 20m.
Samir Patel: I’ll stay on comms and draft an incident stub with timeline. Also tagging @platform-oncall in case we need kernel-level rollback.
Ava Chen: Decision: driver remains Ava Chen until handoff at 22:00 UTC unless regression. Comms owner Samir Patel. Backup Jordan Lee.
Ava Chen: If anyone needs to take over early, reply here. Otherwise I’ll summarize timeline and actions after the monitoring window. :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: Thanks — I’ve noted the handoff plan in the incident stub.
Ava Chen: (final) Monitoring window complete, no regression in last 25m. Moving to reconnaissance -> full postmortem will be created. Handoff: Jordan, you become driver at 22:00 UTC. Samir keeps comms until then.
Jordan Lee: Ack. I’ll take driver at 22:00 and start postmortem draft after.
oncall-bot: INCIDENT UPDATE: P1 status -> mitigated (partial). Next: postmortem required.
Ava Chen: closing thread for active mitigation—moving notes to #incidents/postmortems. Thanks team.

preview:
Maya Patel: handing off now — quick summary before I go offline for the next 8h. Two active pages right now but both mitigated to degraded: 
- INC-4217 (hosted-api latency spike) — mitigation done (fallback to model-v1-hot) and autoscale bumped. Customer impact: visible latency increase for some bursty routes.
- INC-4221 (kv-cache thrash on ded-3) — temporarily pinned cache policy + restart of worker pool; throughput stabilized but we still see elevated 95p.

I'm primary for the next hour while Jon finishes onboarding; after that Jon will be primary, Samir secondary. Ava is on standby for infra escalations.

Jon Rivera: noted. I can take primary at +1h. Can you paste the exact commands you used for the cache pin and the autoscale bump?

Maya Patel: sure — commands I ran in the ops shell: 
```
# bump dedicated pool for hosted-api
redwoodctl capacity adjust --pool hosted-api --gpu-count 4 --min 2 --max 8 --reason "latency spike"
# pin cache policy on ded-3
redwoodctl runtime cache set --node ded-3 --policy pinned --ttl 600
```
Also I applied temporary fallback routing in the policy: 
```
route-policy update hosted-api --fallback model-v1-hot --threshold 1200ms
```

Samir Shah: re: INC-4217 — I filed TKT-9842 to track the customer outreach and billing credits assessment. Will add the perf graphs.

Ava Chen: thanks Samir. On KV thrash, did you check if there was a noisy tenant? last time it was a customer job hitting long sequences.

Maya Patel: yes, ded-3 logs showed repeated long prefix requests from tenant "wallet-svc", ~20K requests in 10m. I flagged their traffic to be rate-limited by policy and DM'd the account manager.

incidents-bot: [ALERT] INC-4221 resolved -> severity downgraded to SEV3. Postmortem required if unresolved within 72h.

Jon Rivera: ok taking primary at 1932104834 (in ~1h). Samir you remain secondary until 1932140000. Ava will handle infra pager if incidents escalate out of runtime scope.

Maya Patel: handoff checklist I completed: 
- Updated incident channels and timelines 
- Bumped capacity and pinned cache (commands above) 
- Filed TKT-9842 for customer comms 
- Noted runbook and playbook links in case notes: https://redwood.internal/runbooks/latency-spike and https://redwood.internal/runbooks/cache-thrash

Jon Rivera: I reviewed runbooks — two quick pointers for next oncall: 
1) If latency spike repeats after fallback, escalate to model-ops for a canary rollback: pager: @model-ops and use `redwoodctl model rollback hosted-api model-v1-hot`
2) If KV thrash persists, scale read-only workers first to drain KV pressure, don't restart primary processes until drains complete.

Samir Shah: action items I'm taking: 
- Add histograms to TKT-9842 for top N tenants by request rate (ETA 3h)
- Schedule a post-handoff async sync for 16:00 PT with product + AMs to coordinate customer comms (I'll create calendar invite)

Maya Patel: thanks. One coverage gap to call out: Ava and I noticed no clear backup for region-eu during night shift — only one oncall rotation currently able to access eu VPC. If Jon is in US timezone tomorrow morning we may have a 4hr window with slow response. Recommend we update the on-call rota or add a daytime EU buddy.

Ava Chen: noted. I can be EU fallback for the next 48h while we update rota, but we should create a short runbook item for cross-region credentials and add to the handoff template.

Jon Rivera: I'll add the EU fallback to the rota and create an RFC in #eng-oncall to make the change permanent. Also I'll ping infra for ephemeral bastion creds for the shift.

Maya Patel: final notes before I log off: 
- Pages to watch: hosted-api (latency), embedding-service (queue depth), dedicated pools (cpu steal)
- Retros: I'll draft a blameless postmortem template for INC-4217 and INC-4221 and drop in #postmortems by EOD.

ops-bot: Deployment notice: rolling restart scheduled on ded-3 at 1932106000 for kernel patch (non-blocking). If impacted, update incident.

Samir Shah: follow-ups: 
- Samir -> TKT-9842: add customer contact + proposed credits (done)
- Jon -> update rota + create RFC (in progress)
- Ava -> add cross-region credentials steps to handoff template (in progress)
- Maya -> postmortem drafts (pending)

Jon Rivera: sounds good. I'll take primary handoff at 1932104834 and post an interim status update if anything new within 30m. If not, I'll confirm quiet status. :thumbsup:

Maya Patel: thanks team, leaving notes and Slack thread pinned. ping me if anything breaks while I'm out. :wave:

title: #eng-oncall: 1919503210
preview:
Marta Rivera: Quick sync — two active incidents right now. Can we lock an EOD triage bridge for handoff? Proposing 6:30pm PT (01:30 UTC). Agenda: 1) quick status for each incident, 2) assign triage lead & escalation contact, 3) decide whether to escalate to infra, 4) capture next steps. INC-512 (latency spike), INC-513 (embedding errors). Who can join?

oncall-bot: INCIDENTS: INC-512 (latency), affected: inference-serving-prod, severity: P2; INC-513 (embed failures), affected: embeddings-api, severity: P2. Links: https://redwood.ops/inc/512 , https://redwood.ops/inc/513

Ava Chen: I can make 6:30pm PT. Happy to take triage lead for INC-513. :thumbsup:

Diego K.: I can cover escalation for INC-512 (oncall until 7pm PT). Will join bridge audio + screen for logs.

Priya Shah: Heads up I have a client call until 6:35pm PT — can join audio only ~6:40pm PT. I can take notes and post dag/rollback decisions.

Marta Rivera: Great — outline so far: Diego escalation contact for INC-512, Ava triage lead for INC-513, Priya taking notes. I'll start the bridge and post runbook links 10m before. If anyone else is available to shadow logs, please RSVP. If no other confirmations by 6:00pm PT I'll start with this crew.

Marta Rivera: Runbook snippets for bridge (paste when we start):
```
# Bridge checklist
open https://redwood.ops/inc/512
open https://redwood.ops/inc/513
./scripts/collect-stacktraces --inc 512 --out /tmp/512-dump.tgz
./scripts/check-embed-queue --inc 513
```

Ava Chen: ack on runbook. I'll queue the embed-replay and share repro logs in thread during call.

Diego K.: I'll pre-warm the query-proxy on the node and watch the latency heatmap. If latency > 95th we escalate to infra and promote canary config.

Marta Rivera: Thanks all — see you on the bridge in ~3m. I'll paste a short agenda in the meeting and tag the oncall runbook.

Priya Shah: joining shortly, will add notes template to the meeting doc.

Ava Chen: :thumbsup:

title: #eng-oncall: 1845002468
preview:
Devon R.: sim-pager: triggering SEV3 drill for incident-comm exercises — mock latency spike on hosted-api. Treat like a real pager, but no customer-impact actions needed. (this is a drill)
Incident Bot: Pager created: SEV3 (drill) — hosted-api / inference-latency. Assigned to oncall @Devon R. — incident id drill-2026-03-15-01. Use #eng-oncall for coordination. (auto)
Devon R.: Acknowledged. Switching my phone to silent but will monitor here. Quick checklist: 1) confirm scope, 2) draft status page update, 3) draft customer note template, 4) prepare internal swarming comms. Who can take status page draft? @Alice Tu? :eyes:
Alice Tu: I can take status page. I'll draft a short update with timeline + impact statement. Will paste below for review before posting.
Ben Carter: I'll prep the customer notification template and approvals path. Also will run the handoff checklist with Sara after 30m. :thumbsup:
Sara Ng: I'll simulate on-call handoff and takeover if you need to step out, Devon. Also running the post-mortem checklist at the end.
Devon R.: Thanks. Current triage: simulated metric spike correlates with a batch of long-context requests; no simulated errors, just latency. No data exfiltration in drill. Tagging this as "Customer-facing but limited" for comms tone.
Alice Tu: Draft status page update (internal review):

```
Summary: We are investigating increased inference latency impacting a subset of requests to hosted API.
Impact: Some requests may experience higher-than-normal latency; errors are not reported.
Scope: A small percentage of traffic routed to model-x with long-context sequences.
Mitigation: Temporarily routing new requests to model-x-light while we investigate.
Next update: in 15 minutes.
```

Alice Tu: pinging for quick reviews — @Ben Carter @Devon R. Quick grammar ok? :mag:
Ben Carter: LGTM. Two small changes: 1) make impact phrasing more action-oriented for status page, 2) add "no customer data exposed" line. After those, I'm fine posting.
Devon R.: Update: reroute applied (simulated) at +4m. Latency metrics trending down in the simulation. Keep status page conservative.
Alice Tu: Final status page ready, posting now to status.redwood.example (DRILL). Copy below for records. (react :white_check_mark:)
Alice Tu: Posted (DRILL): https://status.redwood.example/incidents/drill-2026-03-15-01 (mock link)
Ben Carter: Customer notification template (for review):

```
Subject: Temporary latency increase detected (investigating)

Hello,

We detected a temporary increase in request latency for the hosted API affecting a subset of traffic. Our engineers have applied a temporary routing change to reduce impact while we investigate the root cause. At this time we do not believe any customer data was exposed.

If you see persistent issues or degraded performance, please reply to this message or open a support ticket.

We will provide an update within 1 hour or sooner if the situation changes.

— The Redwood Inference Team
```

Sara Ng: Good: concise, sets expectations. For the drill, we'll send to a small group of internal stakeholders only. Ben, can you add the support escalation link and the dedicated support alias? Also include the expected follow-up cadence (e.g., 1h, 4h, post-mortem)
Ben Carter: Added support alias + cadence. Ready to send when we decide to escalate (drill: internal-only).
Devon R.: Handoff note: I'm going to step out for 10m to grab coffee — Sara can you take the active lead? This is a drill, but treat the handoff as real: update Slack presence and the oncall doc.
Oncall Handoff Bot: Handoff recorded: from Devon R. to Sara Ng — start 1845002520 — reason: short break (drill). Please acknowledge.
Sara Ng: Acknowledged. Taking lead.
Action items I'm running now: 1) monitor post-reroute metrics, 2) confirm no error rates, 3) prep internal bridge notes for eng leads, 4) prepare post-drill feedback survey.
Alice Tu: Internal comms (swarm post) template — posting sample for quick copy: 

"We are currently investigating increased inference latency affecting a subset of requests. Engineers are monitoring and have applied temporary routing changes to mitigate. No customer data exposure detected. Updates every 15 minutes. If you rely on hosted inference for demos or live features, consider switching traffic to dedicated or postponing heavy inference workloads for the next hour." 

Devon R.: Quick note for the drill retrospective: capture timing of detection -> mitigation -> reroute -> status post. Also evaluate who approved the customer wording and the chain for legal/SRE signoff. Documenting now.
Ben Carter: After-action items (for the retro doc):
- Clarify SLA vs. status wording template
- Triage runbook: add explicit step to check long-context request quotas
- Confirm who has authority to post to status page during business hours

Sara Ng: Running simulated post-mortem now. Will run tabletop feedback: 1) Was the initial pager clear? 2) Did comms cadence make sense? 3) Handoff smooth? 4) Any confusion about mitigations? Please add anything else.
Devon R.: Quick feedback: initial pager message was too terse — add brief context and a link to the incident doc in future drills. Also, include a checklist item to mark "DRILL" clearly in every public post.
Alice Tu: Noted. I'll add a short header on the status page and on customer notes explicitly starting with "DRILL — exercise only" to avoid confusion.
Ben Carter: Final: I'll simulate an internal stakeholder email to execs (internal draft) and share in #general later for review.
Draft now: "We executed an incident communications drill to test our status page and customer update templates. No real impact. Findings attached."

Incident Bot: Drill incident closed (drill-2026-03-15-01). Summary: mitigation simulated, status page posted, internal comms exercised. Time to close: ~28 minutes.

Sara Ng: Closing notes: Great run. Please fill the quick feedback form: https://forms.example/drill-feedback (mock). We'll synthesize and publish retro next week. Thanks all. (react :tada:)
Devon R.: Back from break. Acknowledged closed. Good exercise. Filing follow-ups in the oncall-runbook repo.
Alice Tu: Uploading the posted drafts and change suggestions to the incident-comms folder. Will tag people for review.
Ben Carter: Thanks team. Action owners: Ben (customer template + support alias), Alice (status wording + header), Sara (retro synthesis + handoff doc), Devon (runbook updates). Closing thread.

(reacts: multiple :white_check_mark:, :eyes:, :raised_hands:)

title: #eng-oncall: 1798769999
preview:
Ava Chen: Morning team — quick relay before I hand off. I'm primary until 12:30 PT, Samir you're secondary starting 12:00 PT so expect the warm handover around 12:15. Coverage gaps: APAC region has no local secondary during this window, so if we get region-specific pagers ping @ops on-call first.
Samir Patel: noted. I'll be available for escalation and can take primary exactly at 12:30. Planning to check the KV-cache health and node counts at 12:05 to confirm warmstart state.
Lucia Gomez: I can stay on standby for model fallbacks. If a p99 spike happens, please try the model-variant fallback before scaling GPUs.
Ava Chen: Runbooks I recommend pinned for this shift:
- KV cache stall / thrash: https://wiki.redwood.ai/runbooks/kv-cache-stall
- Model fallback + canary rollback: https://wiki.redwood.ai/runbooks/model-fallback
- Region routing & failover: https://wiki.redwood.ai/runbooks/region-failover
Use the 'Shift Handoff' checklist in the console before leaving: Console > Oncall > Shift Handoff.
incident-bot: Pager #INC-2317 — p99 latency spike on hosted cluster us-west-2 (5m avg). Severity: P2. Affected routes: /v1/generate (redwood-large-2). Trigger: token-latency > 1200ms.
Priya Reddy: Confirming—seeing increased inflight and KV cache eviction rate on node group rg-infer-02. CPU fine, GPU memory elevated. Could be prefix cache churn.
Samir Patel: Quick triage steps (do these in order):
1) Check autoscaler status: `kubectl -n inference get hpa,po -l app=inference -o wide`
2) Inspect KV cache hitrate in metrics: Grafana dashboard -> Inference / KV Cache -> last 15m
3) If hitrate < 40% and eviction rate > 200/s, flip to fallback model-variant: `curl -X POST https://controlplane.internal/api/v1/rollouts/fallback -d '{"service":"us-west-2","variant":"quantized-medium"}'`
4) If fallback does not reduce p99 within 2m, scale up dedicated pool: `infractl scale --pool usw2-infer --replicas 3` (ops approval required for > +2 replicas)
Lucia Gomez: If you do fallback, watch quality alerts on Evals -> Live. If regressions > 5% abort and revert.
Ava Chen: If it's a region-only degradation, route traffic to us-east fallback with policy: Console -> Routing -> Temporary Route -> Percentage 30%. Keep ops informed.
ops-bot: Note: scaling requests require a manual approval from SRE leads outside of business hours. Use /approve-scale in the infra channel.
Samir Patel: Quick commands for safe rollbacks:
```
# check active rollout
curl -sS https://controlplane.internal/api/v1/rollouts/active | jq .
# trigger canary rollback
curl -X POST https://controlplane.internal/api/v1/rollouts/rollback -d '{"id":"$(cat /tmp/last-rollout-id)"}'
```
Priya Reddy: Customer impact callout — list of customers hitting us-west: internal `billing-export` shows high QPS from three heavy tenants (acct-441, acct-88, acct-edge). If we escalate to dedicated scaling, flag potential cost overage and notify account teams.
Ava Chen: Communication plan: if p99 > 1.5s for 10m — declare P1 degraded, open incident channel #inc-usw2-latency, call primary pager, and notify sales/CS with the templated message (link: https://wiki.redwood.ai/templates/degraded-service-notice).
Lucia Gomez: Small tip — sometimes prefix cache rebuild helps: touch the warmcache job `kubectl -n infra create job warmcache-$(date +%s) --image=internal/warmcache:latest` only if you trust the job artifacts.

Ava Chen: Morning team — quick relay before I hand off. I'm primary until 12:30 PT, Samir you're secondary starting 12:00 PT so expect the warm handover around 12:15. Coverage gaps: APAC region has no local secondary during this window, so if we get region-specific pagers ping @ops on-call first.

Lucia Gomez: I can stay on standby for model fallbacks. If a p99 spike happens, please try the model-variant fallback before scaling GPUs.
Ava Chen: Runbooks I recommend pinned for this shift:
- KV cache stall / thrash: https://wiki.redwood.ai/runbooks/kv-cache-stall
- Model fallback + canary rollback: https://wiki.redwood.ai/runbooks/model-fallback
- Region routing & failover: https://wiki.redwood.ai/runbooks/region-failover
Use the 'Shift Handoff' checklist in the console before leaving: Console > Oncall > Shift Handoff.
incident-bot: Pager #INC-2317 — p99 latency spike on hosted cluster us-west-2 (5m avg). Severity: P2. Affected routes: /v1/generate (redwood-large-2). Trigger: token-latency > 1200ms.
Priya Reddy: Confirming—seeing increased inflight and KV cache eviction rate on node group rg-infer-02. CPU fine, GPU memory elevated. Could be prefix cache churn.
Samir Patel: Quick triage steps (do these in order):
1) Check autoscaler status: `kubectl -n inference get hpa,po -l app=inference -o wide`
2) Inspect KV cache hitrate in metrics: Grafana dashboard -> Inference / KV Cache -> last 15m
3) If hitrate < 40% and eviction rate > 200/s, flip to fallback model-variant: `curl -X POST https://controlplane.internal/api/v1/rollouts/fallback -d '{"service":"us-west-2","variant":"quantized-medium"}'`
4) If fallback does not reduce p99 within 2m, scale up dedicated pool: `infractl scale --pool usw2-infer --replicas 3` (ops approval required for > +2 replicas)
Lucia Gomez: If you do fallback, watch quality alerts on Evals -> Live. If regressions > 5% abort and revert.
Ava Chen: If it's a region-only degradation, route traffic to us-east fallback with policy: Console -> Routing -> Temporary Route -> Percentage 30%. Keep ops informed.
ops-bot: Note: scaling requests require a manual approval from SRE leads outside of business hours. Use /approve-scale in the infra channel.
Samir Patel: Quick commands for safe rollbacks:
```
# check active rollout
curl -sS https://controlplane.internal/api/v1/rollouts/active | jq .
# trigger canary rollback
curl -X POST https://controlplane.internal/api/v1/rollouts/rollback -d '{"id":"$(cat /tmp/last-rollout-id)"}'
```
Priya Reddy: Customer impact callout — list of customers hitting us-west: internal `billing-export` shows high QPS from three heavy tenants (acct-441, acct-88, acct-edge). If we escalate to dedicated scaling, flag potential cost overage and notify account teams.

preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty alert fired (PD-82419) — p99 latency > 2.5s for model-serving-prod in eu-west-1. Alert rule: p99_latency_route > 2.5s for 3m. Auto-page to eng-oncall.
PagerDuty: Incident PD-82419 created and routed to Priya Nair (on-call).
Priya Nair: acked on PD, jumping in. will check recent deploys + routing.
Jon Becker: on it — looking at autoscaler + queue backlog. seeing CPU stable but GPU queue length up.
Sasha Liu: checking KV cache eviction logs and hotkeys. quick grep shows many evictions around the spike.
Priya Nair: timeline — spike started ~1785012280 per metrics, coincides with a canary rollout of model v2.3.0 about 6m ago.
Jon Becker: suspects rollout + batch config mismatch causing tail latency. reverting canary?
Priya Nair: not yet — let's try mitigation first: scale dedicated pool and reduce max_batch_size for v2.3.0. If that fails, rollback.
OpsBot: runbook link added: https://redwood/runbooks/latency-tail-spike — checklist appended to thread.
Sasha Liu: snippet from kvcache logs:
```
2026-04-05T12:10:11Z WARN kvcache eviction key=customer:hotkey:12345 size=482MB
2026-04-05T12:10:12Z INFO kvcache miss key=customer:hotkey:12345
2026-04-05T12:10:13Z ERROR serve latency_ms=2791 req_id=abcd1234
```
Jon Becker: running command to bump capacity in eu-west-1: `infractl pool resize --pool model-serving-prod-eu --add 4-gpu`
Priya Nair: doing a quick config patch to force max_batch_size=8 for v2.3.0 in canary. patch applied to 2 nodes. monitoring.
PagerDuty: PD-82419 acknowledged by Priya Nair. Escalation paused.
Sasha Liu: checking customer-facing impact — dashboards show 0.8% of requests above 2.5s in last 5m, concentrated on a small set of API keys (hotkey pattern).
Ben Carter: support here — if it's only a subset, we can route those keys to fallback model variant until we stabilize. drafting message to affected accounts.

Sasha Liu: done. rule applied and propagated to edge within ~45s.

Sofia Morales: I can take 14:30–18:00 PT if that works. I'll mark myself primary in PD and leave runbook pointers here.

```

title: #eng-oncall: 1772223333
preview:
incident-bot: PagerDuty alert triggered: PD-INV-18872 - inference-proxy edge 5xx burst (threshold: 5m error rate > 4%). Link: <https://pagerduty.com/incidents/PD-INV-18872>
Ava Chen: acked — I'm oncall, investigating. pinging infra to check autoscaler and edge pool.
Sam Torres: seeing a 3–4 minute spike on router-503s for us-west-2, error-rate jump from 0.3% -> 3.6%. latency p95 unchanged. pulling traces now.
Priya Rao: FYI PD escalated to @ops-escalation at +2min. any customer-facing 5xx counts?
Sam Torres: quick query against prod-logs: most 5xx are from internal probes / synthetic checks hitting a new health endpoint. example log line: `GET /_healthcheck 500 from 10.12.8.9`
Luca Bianchi: hm looks like the synthetic runner hit a bad binary and started spamming probes. I've temporarily added a predicate to drop probe traffic at the proxy: `edge.filter.drop_tag == "synth-check"` — rolling at 3 hosts first.
Ava Chen: good call. please comment when rule is live. I'll keep PD acked and watch.
Luca Bianchi: rule is live on canary (host-12,13,14) — error-rate on those hosts dropped to baseline. rolling to remaining hosts in 60s.
pagerduty-bot: Incident PD-INV-18872 acknowledged by Ava Chen.
Sam Torres: dashboards show overall cluster 5xx returning to normal as roll continues. p95 latency steady. looks like noisy-synthetic causing flapping alerts not real customer impact.
Priya Rao: can we silence PD for the alert group until the synthetic owner rolls a fix? otherwise we keep getting pages.
Ava Chen: yes please. applying soft-silence for the alert rule 10m while we verify.
incident-bot: Soft-silence applied to alert group `edge-5xx-spike` for 10m by Ava Chen.
pagerduty-bot: PagerDuty note: alert auto-resolve window started (no further triggers).
Sam Torres: confirm no 5xx spikes in real user traffic (prod-edge logs filtered by UA not containing "synth"): nothing substantial in last 10m.
Luca Bianchi: created a follow-up ticket: INF-4891 to roll back bad synthetic and add better retires on the runner.
Priya Rao: action item: reduce synthetic cadence to avoid bulk probe bursts. assigned to synthetic infra.
Ava Chen: closing thread — alert auto-resolved, PD incident closed (auto-resolve). No customer impact determined. If it flaps again we'll escalate to oncall rotation and do full postmortem. :thumbsup:
pagerduty-bot: Incident PD-INV-18872 automatically resolved (status: resolved)
incident-bot: Note: this thread logged as a transient flapping page caused by synthetic probe noise. Recommend follow-up with synthetic team and consider adding a filter to the alert to ignore known-probe IPs.
Priya Rao: I'll open a short blameless note and attach graphs. tagging @synth-team.
Sam Torres: TL;DR: transient synthetic probe loop produced bursty 5xx; quick in-line edge filter + soft-silence mitigated; no customer impact; INF-4891 created.
Ava Chen: thanks all — monitoring for 30m then will hand back to normal rotation.

title: #eng-oncall: 1772045120
preview:
Rafael Mendes: want to sync on a recurring point of confusion: when does Support move an escalation from #support into #incidents vs keeping it in #support + paging oncall? seeing a few cases where we ended up with 2 parallel threads.

Dev Patel: +1. Support’s default is “start in #support, page if P0/P1”, but sometimes oncall immediately asks us to move it. would love a rule of thumb we can paste into the playbook.

Owen Phillips: from Support side: we’re trying not to spam #incidents for single-customer stuff, but we also don’t want to hide a real incident. examples we’ve struggled with: streaming resets (Acme, us-east) vs “unexpected 429 after SDK upgrade” (Aurora).

Sean Gallagher: agree we need a clean trigger. #incidents should be for anything that looks like platform degradation OR will require coordinated mitigation/comms. #support is fine for “help me debug this customer” cases.

Mina Haddad: +1. especially for routing/fallback questions—Support often needs quick confirmation of “is fallback active?” that can stay in #support if it’s just interpretation. but if fallback is activating unexpectedly at scale, that’s incident territory.

Rafael Mendes: concrete proposal:
1) start in #support for single-account triage / suspected client-side behavior
2) move to #incidents when any of the below is true:
- suspected regional/service degradation (elevated 5xx, latency, timeouts) even if only 1 customer reported so far
- multiple customers or multiple tickets reporting same symptom in short window
- needs an oncall-led mitigation (traffic shift, rollback, capacity move, config change)
- needs status page / broad comms decision
- exceeds paging threshold (aligned to SLO alerts) OR we’re going to page more than one team

Priya Shah: can we add “Private installer upgrades stuck” as an example? typically 1 customer, but requires a coordinated eng response + careful rollback steps.

Sean Gallagher: for Private: if it’s one customer blocked on upgrade, that’s usually #support + page the Private Deployments oncall (not necessarily #incidents) unless it’s a release-wide regression or we expect more customers to hit it.

Owen Phillips: makes sense. so “customer-specific but high touch” != automatically #incidents.

Dev Patel: what about embedded timeouts eu-west (like SUP-1850-style)? started as single customer, but ended up being partial degradation.

Rafael Mendes: that’s a good “start in #support but move fast” case. if the first triage checks show signals outside the customer (dashboards, error rate, synthetic), we should move to #incidents early. we lose time when we wait for “2nd customer confirmation.”

Mina Haddad: +1. also, for routing/fallback: if Support sees pinned model “ignored” (SUP-1847-type), that can stay in #support until we confirm it’s widespread. if we see unexpected fallback activations or policy violations across customers/tiers, #incidents.

Dylan Brooks: tooling angle: if we do move to #incidents, can we standardize the handoff? like: link SUP ticket(s), copy the incident template, pin request IDs + region + model. otherwise #incidents becomes scrollback archaeology.

Rafael Mendes: yes. we’re already adding “required context” for pages; same should apply for moving to #incidents.

incident-bot: Reminder: For active incidents, use /incident start in #incidents to create an incident record and status updates. (If you started in another channel, link it here.)

Owen Phillips: ok but can we be explicit about what *doesn’t* go to #incidents? we’ve had oncall ask us to move things that are basically docs / SDK troubleshooting.

Sean Gallagher: examples that should stay in #support:
- 429/quota confusion after SDK upgrade (until proven backend regression)
- structured output validation errors / schema issues
- SSO login loop for one tenant (unless auth provider outage)
- webhook SSL validation failures where it’s customer cert chain

Dev Patel: and examples that should go to #incidents:
- streaming disconnects spiking in a region (even if only 1 enterprise customer notices first)
- rerank endpoint 500s on large batches if we see elevated 5xx
- “console dashboards missing regions” only if telemetry pipeline is broken for multiple customers

Mina Haddad: one nuance: #incidents isn’t the same as “page SRE”. we can have an incident w/ Platform owning. but #incidents is still the right place when we need one shared source of truth + comms.

Rafael Mendes: exactly. #incidents is coordination + decision log. paging is separate.

Priya Shah: what about update cadence? Support sometimes keeps it in #support because they’re doing customer updates and don’t want to overcomplicate.

Sean Gallagher: if it’s in #incidents, it actually helps. we can align: who is customer-facing writer, what’s approved to say, and when to post status page vs docs banner.

Dev Patel: ok I’m sold. can we write the “move to #incidents” criteria in one pasteable block?

Rafael Mendes: draft pasteable guidance (feel free to edit):
“Keep in #support when: it’s a single-customer issue with no evidence of platform degradation; suspected client config/SDK usage; needs targeted debugging.
Move to #incidents when: suspected service/regional degradation; multiple customers affected; mitigation requires coordinated eng action (rollback/traffic shift/capacity); we may post a status update or need cross-team coordination. When moving: link all SUP tickets, paste incident context (customer/tier, region, model, request IDs, start time, impact), and pin it.”

Owen Phillips: :thumbsup: add one line: “Don’t wait for 2nd customer if dashboards show it.”

Rafael Mendes: +1. adding.

Dylan Brooks: I’ll align Slack workflow with this. INT-332/INT-333 already tracking the automation; we can add a step that prompts “Move to #incidents? (Y/N)” w/ the criteria + link to template.

Dev Patel: I’ll update the escalation playbook section + share in #support. also will add the examples list (429 vs streaming vs private upgrade).

Sean Gallagher: please include the note that #incidents is not a backdoor to bypass incident process. once it’s there, we should use /incident if it’s truly incident-worthy; otherwise keep it in #support.

Mina Haddad: and for routing-specific escalations, add “if fallback is active for a single customer, confirm in #support; if fallback activation is unexpected or widespread, move to #incidents.”

Rafael Mendes: cool. I’ll post a summary and we’ll treat this as the default.

Rafael Mendes: SUMMARY / agreement:
- Default start: #support for triage.
- Move to #incidents when there’s evidence/suspicion of platform or regional degradation, multi-customer impact, need for coordinated mitigation/comms, or cross-team paging.
- Don’t wait for a second report if dashboards/synthetics indicate an issue.
- Private “single customer blocked” stays in #support unless it’s a release-wide regression.
- When moving to #incidents: link SUP tickets + paste/pin context (region/model/request IDs/impact) and use /incident when appropriate.

Dev Patel: :thumbsup: will incorporate verbatim.
